options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Determines the goal of the slot. Some goals are specific to certain campaigns
Options starting with "Castle" are played in the Castle Hammerwatch campaign, while "Temple" options are played in the Temple of the Sun Campaign
Castle All Bosses: Defeat all the bosses in Castle Hammerwatch. Escaping is NOT required
Castle Escape: Find at least 12 Strange Planks, defeat Worldfire, and escape with your life
Castle Plank Hunt: Find a certain number of Strange Planks in Castle Hammerwatch
Temple All Bosses: Defeat all the bosses in the Temple of the Sun
Temple Plank Hunt: Find a certain number of Strange Planks in the Temple of the Sun
Temple Pyramid of Fear: Unlock and complete the Pyramid of Fear

options.name: planks_required_count
options.description: Determines the amount of Strange Planks required to win the game for the Plank Hunt goals

options.name: extra_plank_percent
options.description:
Determines the percentage of extra Strange Planks in the item pool
For the Castle Escape goal, the required planks count is 12
For the Plank Hunt goals, the required planks count is determined by the Plank Hunt Requirement setting

options.name: shopsanity_p1
options.description:
Adds shop upgrades of this player's class to the item pool and enables items to appear in their shop
All members of the same class will receive their upgrades together and will have the same items in their shops
WARNING: there is normally not enough gold to purchase all upgrades.
It is strongly recommended to reduce the costs of shop items so you will not have to grind excessively for gold!
WARNING #2: there is no extra logic for shop upgrades!
This means you could get into a situation where you are required to play much of your game with no upgrades.
Consider making shop upgrades local if you're playing in a larger multiworld

options.name: shopsanity_p2
options.description:
Adds shop upgrades of this player's class to the item pool and enables items to appear in their shop
All members of the same class will receive their upgrades together and will have the same items in their shops
WARNING: see the warnings in the Shopsanity Player 1 Class option!

options.name: shopsanity_p3
options.description:
Adds shop upgrades of this player's class to the item pool and enables items to appear in their shop
All members of the same class will receive their upgrades together and will have the same items in their shops
WARNING: see the warnings in the Shopsanity Player 1 Class option!

options.name: shopsanity_p4
options.description:
Adds shop upgrades of this player's class to the item pool and enables items to appear in their shop
All members of the same class will receive their upgrades together and will have the same items in their shops
WARNING: see the warnings in the Shopsanity Player 1 Class option!

options.name: bonus_behavior
options.description:
Determines how bonus chest locations in bonus levels are handled
None: Don't include any bonus chest items/locations
Necessary: Include bonus level locations for any extra items in the pool
All: Include all bonus chest items/locations. Extra items will replace filler items as normal

options.name: randomize_bonus_keys
options.description: Whether bonus keys are shuffled into the pool

options.name: remove_lives
options.description:
Removes extra lives (Ankhs) from the item pool and replaces them with filler.
Recommended to have enabled when playing with the infinite_lives or no_extra_lives modifiers

options.name: randomize_recovery_items
options.description: Whether recovery items (such as apples and mana crystals) are shuffled into the pool

options.name: randomize_secrets
options.description: (TotS only) Whether items from random secrets (small rooms with cracked walls in the cave levels) are shuffled into the item pool

options.name: randomize_puzzles
options.description: Whether items from peg puzzles are shuffled into the item pool

options.name: randomize_enemy_loot
options.description:
Whether items dropped by minibosses and towers are shuffled into the item pool
On: major enemy loot locations will exist using the vanilla loot drop chances
All: forces all major enemy loot locations to exist and have items

options.name: buttonsanity
options.description:
Whether the effects of buttons and switches are shuffled into the item pool
Normal: button/switch effects are turned into locations, so completing a button sequence or puzzle is one location
Insanity: all buttons/switches will be locations, regardless if the button would do anything by itself or not

options.name: key_mode
options.description:
Changes the behavior of how keys interact with gates
Generic: keys can be used anywhere on their respective gates (WARNING: can cause generation failures!)
Act Specific: (castle only) replaces keys with versions that can only be used in a specific act
Floor Master: consolidates keys on a floor into a single item that unlocks all gates of that type on an entire floor

options.name: extra_keys_percent
options.description: Determines the percentage of extra silver and gold keys (and mirrors in the TotS campaign) that are added to the item pool

options.name: big_bronze_key_percent
options.description: (Castle only) What percentage of bronze keys get converted into big bronze keys, which act as 3 bronze keys each

options.name: pan_fragments
options.description:
(TotS only) If greater than 1 separates the pan into multiple fragments
All fragments must be collected in order to purchase from the consumables merchant

options.name: lever_fragments
options.description:
(TotS only) Determines if/how the pumps lever is split into multiple items
If 0 or 'per_level' the pumps lever will be split into 3 items that only lower the water on a specific cave level
If greater than 1 separates the pumps lever into multiple fragments
All fragments must be collected in order to turn on all the pumps

options.name: pickaxe_fragments
options.description:
(TotS only) If greater than 1 separates the pickaxe into multiple fragments
All fragments must be collected in order for the rocks outside the temple to be broken

options.name: hammer_fragments
options.description:
If not 0 makes fragile walls unbreakable without receiving a custom hammer item
If greater than 1 separates the hammer into multiple fragments
All fragments must be collected in order to break down fragile walls

options.name: trap_item_percent
options.description: What percentage of filler items are replaced with traps

options.name: trap_item_weights
options.description: The relative weights of trap items in the item pool

options.name: exit_randomization
options.description:
Randomizes where level exits and portals lead
No Boss Exits: exits to boss levels will not be shuffled
All: all exits including bosses will be shuffled
WARNING: This option has a moderate chance of causing generation to fail, use at your own risk!

options.name: er_act_range
options.description:
Determines the maximum number of acts away levels will try to be connected to each other in exit randomization
For example with an act range of 1 Prison exits will mostly only connect to other Prison exits or Armory exits

options.name: random_start_exit
options.description:
If Exit Randomization is on, you will start the game at a random exit determined by the Start Location Act option
Use the /t command in game to return if you get stuck!

options.name: random_start_exit_act
options.description: Determines the act in which you start if Randomize Start Location is on

options.name: gate_shuffle
options.description: Shuffles whether gates are bronze, silver, or gold

options.name: shop_shuffle
options.description: Shuffles the shops around so that they may be different from their normal locations

options.name: enemy_shuffle_mode
options.description:
Shuffles the locations of enemies, spawners, mini-bosses, and towers in each level
Individual: enemies will be shuffled individually regardless of type, keeping the same ratios as vanilla
Type: converts all enemies of a single type into another. For example all tier 1 bats can become tier 1 beetles
Chaos: enemies will be shuffled individually regardless of type or ratio

options.name: enemy_shuffle_act_range
options.description:
How many acts away enemies can be shuffled to
When set to 0 only enemies in the same act will be shuffled
When set to 3 enemies can be shuffled to any act

options.name: enemy_shuffle_keep_tier
options.description: If Enemy Shuffle is on, only shuffles enemies of the same tier amongst each other

options.name: open_castle_portals
options.description:
(Castle only) Starts the game with the specified number of portals open in the hub
This starts with the prison portal and goes up to the chambers portal

options.name: shortcut_teleporter
options.description:
(Castle only) Enables the use of the shortcut portal at the beginning of the game. This allows early access
to Prison Floor 3 before the bronze gate, preventing a potential early BK
A return portal will also be placed so that the first two floors can be fully completed

options.name: treasure_shuffle
options.description: Shuffles the locations of minor treasure such as coins, crates, pots, and vegetation

options.name: er_seed
options.description: Determines the seed for generating the exit randomization layout. If "random" the seed will be random

options.name: difficulty
options.description: What difficulty the game will be played on

options.name: shop_upgrade_category_shuffle
options.description:
Shuffles the shop upgrades between different categories of shops
Group: upgrades sharing the same base name (Damage 1, 2, 3, etc.) are shuffled together in the same category
All: upgrades are shuffled amongst all categories, regardless of prerequisites

options.name: shop_cost_min
options.description: The lowest percent each shop upgrade cost can be multiplied by

options.name: shop_cost_max
options.description: The highest percent each shop upgrade cost can be multiplied by

options.name: shopsanity_trap_obfuscation
options.description:
Cause the names and descriptions of trap items in shops to mimic another item in the world of the receiving player
On: trap names/descriptions will mimic another item
Imperfect: trap names/descriptions will mimic another item, but with subtle changes in letters/punctuation
L33T: 7r4p n4m35/d35cr1p710n5 w1ll b3 1n 4ll l3375p34k
OwO: twap names/descwiptions wiww be in aww owo
All Mystery: instead hides ALL item names/descriptions, so none of the items will be known!

options.name: shopsanity_balance_costs
options.description:
Reduces the costs of items in shops based on their classifications
If the item is not progression it will cost 50% as much, and filler items will cost 10% as much

options.name: shopsanity_assist
options.description:
Various methods to make base shop upgrades more accessible
Duplicate Base Upgrades: add an extra copy of each base upgrade to the item pool
Swap Base Upgrades: swap base upgrades with the earliest version of its sub upgrade so you should find base upgrades first
only considers Hammerwatch locations
Swap Base Upgrades Experimental: like Swap Base Upgrades, but will swap with locations in ANY game. This may cause issues with other apworlds!

options.name: portal_accessibility
options.description:
(TotS only) Ensures rune keys will be placed locally on the floor they would normally appear so that hub portals
are more easily accessible

options.name: no_sunbeam_damage
options.description: (TotS only) Disables sunbeam damage, removing much of the long and awkward backtracking inside the temple

options.name: starting_life_count
options.description: How many extra lives each player will start the game with

options.name: game_modifiers
options.description:
Enforces game modifiers to be set either true or false
Valid modifiers are: no_extra_lives, 1_hp, shared_hp_pool, no_hp_pickups, no_mana_regen, reverse_hp_regen,
infinite_lives, hp_regen, double_damage, double_lives, and 5x_mana_regen

options.name: death_link
options.description:
When anybody dies, everyone dies. This also applies to all multiplayer players within a single game
This can be changed later in game in the Archipelago options menu
